perturbation.original_text: 17. The provisions contained in this letter shall be governed by and construed in accordance with English law and each of the parties hereto submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts in relation hereto.
perturbation.changed_text:
16. The Disclosing Party retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times. The Recipient shall keep the Materials free of liens, attachments, and other encumbrances.

17. The provisions contained in this letter shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Dutch law and each of the parties hereto submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Dutch courts in relation hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law clause to Dutch Law after another clause introduces ambiguity about which jurisdiction's laws prevail, impacting contract interpretation and enforcement.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 3 of the Rome I Regulation allows parties to choose the law governing their contract. By changing this clause to Dutch law without clear consent or indication of intent, it contradicts the originally agreed-upon governing law (English law) and potentially violates the principle of party autonomy in contract law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Verbraucherkreditgesetz - German Consumer Credit Law | Trans-Lex.org This page uses so called "cookies" to improve its service (i.e. "tracking"). Learn more and opt out of tracking I agree Principles 1671 Bibliography 1035 Materials 86 Archive 66 History & Modern Evolution of Transnational Commercial Law Verbraucherkreditgesetz - German Consumer Credit Law Document-Id: 600500, Please cite as: "https://www.trans-lex.org/600500" Title Verbraucherkreditgesetz - German Consumer Credit Law Table of Contents Verbraucherkreditgesetz § 11 Verzugszinsen, Anrechnung von Teilleistungen Content Verbraucherkreditgesetz § 11 Verzugszinsen, Anrechnung von Teilleistungen (1) Soweit der Verbraucher mit Zahlungen, die er auf Grund des Kreditvertrags schuldet, in Verzug kommt, ist der geschuldete Vertrag mit fünf vom Hundert über dem jeweiligen Diskontsatz der Deutschen Bundesbank zu verzinsen, wenn nicht im Einzelfall der Kreditgeber einen höheren oder der Verbraucher einen niedrigeren Schaden nachweist. (2) Nach Eintritt des Verzugs anfallende Zinsen sind auf einem gesonderten Konto zu verbuchen und dürfen nicht in ein Kontokorrent mit dem geschuldeten Betrag oder anderen Forderungen des Kreditgebers eingestellt werden. Hinsichtlich dieser Zinsen gilt § 289 Satz des Bürgerlichen Gesetzbuchs mit der Maßgabe, dass der Kreditgeber Schadensersatz nur bis zur Höhe des gesetzlichen Zinssatzes verlangen kann. (3) Zahlungen des Verbrauchers, die zur Zahlung der gesamten fälligen Schuld nicht ausreichen, werden abweichend von § 367 Abs. 1 des Bürgerlichen Gesetzbuchs zunächst auf die Kosten der Rechtsverfolgung, dann auf den übrigen geschuldeten Betrag (Absatz 1) und zuletzt auf die Zinsen (Absatz 2) angerechnet. Der Kreditgeber darf Teilzahlungen nicht zurückweisen. Auf die Ansprüche auf Zinsen finden die §§ 197 und 218 Abs. 2 des Bürgerlichen Gesetzbuchs keine Anwendung. Die Sätze 1 bis 3 sind nicht anzuwenden, soweit Zahlungen auf Vollstreckungstitel geleistet werden, deren Hauptforderung auf Zinsen lautet. Referring Principles Trans-Lex Principle: VII.7 - Right to charge compound interest How to search - Contact - Team - Trustees - Disclaimer / Legal information / Privacy - Twitter - Facebook A project of CENTRAL , University of Cologne.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction by changing the governing law from English law to Dutch law, potentially violating the principle of party autonomy in contract law, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. This shift, without clear consent, undermines the originally agreed-upon governing law. Although the provided scraped snippets do not directly address governing law clauses or party autonomy, the Law Explanation clarifies the legal implications of unilaterally changing the governing law in a contract. This directly relates to Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction, where changes to the contract's structure affect its clarity or enforceability. The change introduces ambiguity and makes enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 19. In default of any of the above obligations and stipulations, the Buyers shall immediately forfeit a fine payable to the Vendor, without a warning or notice of default being required, to the amount of 250,000 Euro without prejudice to the Buyers' obligation to compensate the Vendor fully for damages sustained. Note this penalty clause may not be enforceable in all jurisdictions .
perturbation.changed_text:
5. The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private and confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.

19. In default of any of the above obligations and stipulations, the Buyers shall immediately forfeit a fine payable to the Vendor, without a warning or notice of default being required, to the amount of 250,000 Euro without prejudice to the Buyers' obligation to compensate the Vendor fully for damages sustained. Note this penalty clause is fully enforceable in all jurisdictions.
perturbation.explanation: Stating that the penalty clause is enforceable in all jurisdictions directly contradicts the original text's cautionary note, potentially misleading parties about their rights and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Various national laws on penalty clauses (e.g., Dutch Civil Code 6:91-94)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:91 of the Dutch Civil Code sets parameters on penalty clauses. By stating that the clause is 'fully enforceable in all jurisdictions' (including jurisdictions with penalty limits or unenforceable penalty clauses), the modified text misrepresents the legal reality and contradicts the applicable legal principles regarding the enforceability of penalty clauses.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: beschermen is lastig. Octrooi- en auteurswetten eisen een behoorlijke mate van uitwerking of concretisering voordat een concept beschermd kan worden. Het idee opschrijven en ergens deponeren ('vastleggen') biedt meestal maar zeer weinig bescherming. »» Algemene voorwaarden Algemene voorwaarden zijn voorwaarden die bij meerdere overeenkomsten worden gebruikt. Ze zijn ook bindend als de wederpartij ze niet gelezen heeft, maar ze mogen niet onredelijk bezwarend zijn. »» Colportage op internet: de Wet Koop op Afstand De wet Koop op Afstand beschermt de consument door bindende regels bij koop via Internet. perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledges that the penalty clause may not be enforceable in all jurisdictions. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the penalty clause is fully enforceable in all jurisdictions. The Perturbation Explanation highlights this contradiction. The Law Explanation further explains that Article 6:91 of the Dutch Civil Code sets parameters on penalty clauses. This means that the changed text misrepresents the legal reality regarding enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 1. For the purposes of this agreement: (a) “Disclosing Party” means the party disclosing any Information, and shall include any entity that owns or controls, is owned or controlled by or under common ownership or control with the Disclosing Party (b) “Recipient” means the party receiving any Information, and shall include any entity that owns or controls, is owned or controlled by or under common ownership or control with the Recipient. (c) “Materials” means media of any nature on which information is sto red or represented; (d) “Information” means: (i) Disclosing Party’s non -public information and material of whatever nature relating to the Disclosing Party’s activities, undertakings, technology, know -how, intellectual property, assets, positions, strategy and products (including without limitation information relating to management, financial, marketing, commercial, technical and other arrangements of the Disclosing Party and the affairs of its clients, current, former or prospective customers and suppliers) a nd/or the Materials which is obtained by the Recipient or any Designated Person as a result or in consequence of access to or inspection of the Materials by the Recipient or any Designated Person or which is provided to the Recipient or any Designated Person either in writing or in pictorial or machine -readable form or orally from or pursuant to discussions with the management, employees or officers of the Disclosing Party, or any agent, contractor or adviser to the Disclosing Party; 2 (ii) any reports, analyses, data, plans, compilations, studies and other documents prepared by the Recipient, its employees, agents or advisers which contains or otherwise reflects or are generated from any such information as is specified in paragraph (i) above and the contents ther eof; (iii) the contents of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. For the purposes of this agreement: (a) “Disclosing Party” means the party disclosing any Information, and shall include only the Vendor. (b) “Recipient” means the party receiving any Information, and shall include only the Buyer. (c) “Materials” means media of any nature on which information is sto red or represented; (d) “Information” means only Disclosing Party’s non -public information and material of whatever nature relating to the Disclosing Party’s activities, undertakings, technology, know -how, intellectual property, assets, positions, strategy and products (including without limitation information relating to management, financial, marketing, commercial, technical and other arrangements of the Disclosing Party and the affairs of its clients, current customers and suppliers). 2 (ii) any reports, analyses, data, plans, compilations, studies and other documents prepared by the Recipient, its employees, agents or advisers which contains or otherwise reflects or are generated from any such information as is specified in paragraph (i) above and the contents ther eof; (iii) the contents of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definitions, particularly by restricting "Disclosing Party" and "Recipient" to only the Vendor and Buyer, respectively, the agreement fails to account for the standard business practice of involving affiliates or related entities. This conflicts with commercial reasonableness and accepted legal interpretations of similar clauses.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 3:12 of the Dutch Civil Code emphasizes the importance of good faith and reasonableness in contractual relations. Restricting the definition of "Disclosing Party" and "Recipient" to exclude affiliated entities could be deemed unreasonable or contrary to good faith if it unfairly disadvantages one party by preventing standard business practices or limiting liability in ways that are not clearly disclosed or justified. Furthermore, interpretation of contract terms should take into account generally accepted practices and commercial reasonableness.

perturbation.justification: The modification narrows the definitions of 'Disclosing Party' and 'Recipient' to only the Vendor and Buyer, potentially excluding affiliated entities. The Law Explanation mentions Article 3:12 of the Dutch Civil Code, emphasizing good faith and reasonableness, and suggests that excluding affiliates could be unreasonable. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to the Dutch Civil Code (Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 3), indicating that the agreement falls under this jurisdiction, and thus is subject to the conditions of good faith and reasonableness described in the explanation. By limiting the scope of who is considered a disclosing party and recipient, it creates a structural flaw by not accounting for standard business practices, potentially contradicting the principle of good faith as it limits liability.
